drafts.title: Sediment is LLM curator plus minimal hard gates, not a mechanical extractor
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment's role is LLM curator plus minimal hard safety/storage substrate -- not a mechanical gate extractor. Semantic judgments (what merits recording, what kind, what status, what confidence, whether maxim) belong entirely to the LLM based on conversational context and retrieval results. All Phase 1.4 mechanical gates -- forceProvisional, disallowMaxim, maxConfidence, disallowArchived, G13 near-duplicate, readiness dry-run, rolling pass-rate, rate/sampling -- have been permanently deleted (not disabled or configurable; gone). The only non-LLM logic retained: (a) deterministic secret sanitizer before any LLM exposure, and (b) storage integrity gates (path sandbox, schema parse/lint, lock, atomic write, audit, git). Everything else is the curator LLM's domain.

drafts.title: Curator operation priority: update/merge first, create second, delete last
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment curator must follow this operation priority: UPDATE or MERGE existing memory first; then SUPERSEDE or ARCHIVE stale memory; then CREATE new memory; DELETE only as last resort for secrets, junk, or explicit user request. Skip is always valid when no durable value is gained. Defaulting to CREATE without checking for existing entries is a bug. Full operation set: create / update / merge / supersede / archive / delete / skip. Merge updates the target entry compiled truth plus derives_from, then archives non-target sources. Supersede supports old_slug plus optional existing new_slug but does not auto-create the replacement entry.

drafts.title: Curator must call memory_search before any create to find semantic neighbors
drafts.compiledTruth: Before any create operation the sediment curator must call memory_search to retrieve semantic neighbors of the candidate knowledge, then read the full content of top candidates. Based on that lookup it decides: update, merge, supersede, archive, skip, or create. Creating without this lookup is a defect that produces duplicate or conflicting entries and defeats knowledge self-evolution. memory_search must be LLM retrieval -- not grep or BM25 fallback -- because correct curator decisions depend on semantic accuracy. If memory_search is unavailable (hard error), the create must not proceed using degraded retrieval results.

drafts.title: Credential sanitizer uses typed redaction and continues; does not fail-closed on pattern match
drafts.compiledTruth: From 2026-05-15 (revised from earlier fail-closed behavior): when the deterministic sanitizer detects a secret, token, private key, JWT, or credential URL, it replaces it with a typed placeholder [SECRET:<type>] and the extraction pipeline continues. It does NOT fail-closed for the entire extraction round. Fail-closed is only triggered if the sanitizer itself encounters an unrecoverable internal error. Raw secrets must never cross the LLM, audit, or memory boundary under any circumstance -- the typed placeholder preserves surrounding context while enforcing the boundary. Applies in auto-write lane, curator, and explicit-extract paths.

drafts.title: Sediment retains exactly two categories of hard non-LLM gates after curator migration
drafts.compiledTruth: After the LLM curator migration, sediment retains exactly two categories of hard non-LLM gates. Category 1 -- sensitive information boundary: deterministic sanitizer replaces secrets/tokens/keys/credential URLs with [SECRET:<type>] before any LLM or memory exposure; fail-closed only on sanitizer internal error, not on pattern match. Category 2 -- storage integrity: path sandbox, schema parse, lint error, write lock, atomic write, audit trail, git commit. All other gate logic (duplicate detection, confidence caps, kind bans, readiness dry-run, rolling pass-rate, sampling stride) has been permanently removed. There is no configuration to re-enable them.

drafts.title: Second brain knowledge base is self-evolving, not append-only
drafts.compiledTruth: The knowledge base is self-evolving: existing entries can be updated with better evidence, merged when two entries cover the same topic, superseded when a decision reverses, archived when stale, or deleted when wrong. The maintenance contract is 'current best compiled truth per topic,' not 'append all observations as parallel entries.' Append-only accumulation is an explicit failure mode: old decisions coexist with new facts, forcing retrieval to resolve contradictions that should have been resolved at write time. Any curator behavior that consistently produces parallel near-duplicate entries instead of updating is a defect to be corrected via curator prompt, not a gate.

drafts.title: memory_update and memory_delete must not be exposed as LLM-facing tools in main session
drafts.compiledTruth: memory_update and memory_delete are sediment sidecar internal capabilities only. They must not be registered as LLM-facing tools in the main session. Main session memory_* tools are permanently read-only (search, get, neighbors, decide). The sediment sidecar is the sole authorized writer to the knowledge base. Exposing write tools to the main session LLM creates a prompt-injection attack surface: adversarial content in conversation could trigger unsanctioned writes or deletions. This boundary is not configurable and not subject to per-project override.

drafts.title: Curator deletes default to soft mode; hard delete only for secrets, junk, or explicit user request
drafts.compiledTruth: Delete operations in the curator default to soft mode: the entry is marked archived with a timeline note rather than physically removed from the store. Hard delete (permanent removal) is reserved for exactly three cases: (1) the entry contains a secret or credential that bypassed sanitizer, (2) the entry is obvious junk such as test noise or malformed fragments, (3) the user explicitly requests removal of a specific entry. Git history is the rollback surface for all write operations including deletes. Soft-default preserves recoverability in the face of curator errors without requiring user review workflows.
